summary: DHH announces his proposal to speak about Rails at the fourth annual RubyConf in October 2004. He shares his talk abstract, which focuses on how Rails grew from real-world problems while maintaining a generic core. He also mentions an upcoming talk about building Basecamp in Chicago and expresses interest in speaking at the JAOO conference in Denmark.
key_points: DHH submitted a proposal for a full-length Rails talk at RubyConf 2004, marking early efforts to evangelize the framework
The talk abstract emphasizes Rails' origin in real problems rather than abstract design, a philosophy DHH would champion for decades
DHH was actively seeking multiple speaking opportunities across the US and Europe to build Rails' audience
money_quotes: How Rails was grown on a diet of real problems and influences, attracted an audience by being concrete, and still kept a generic core.
key_insight: This early 2004 post captures DHH in full grassroots evangelism mode, pitching Rails at every conference he can find while articulating the framework's core philosophy of growing from real problems rather than theoretical design.
